findings.issue: The draft contradicts its own stated domain for the square-root example.
findings.evidence: Example 2.5 defines the example as “f(x)=√x, x>0”, but Remark 2.3 says “the original function is defined at x=0 (with f(0)=0)”. For the function as declared in the example and seed, 0 is not in the domain.
findings.suggestion: Keep the seed’s domain restriction and say the derivative formula is defined for x>0, or explicitly redefine a separate natural-domain example before discussing x=0.

findings.where: Reading f′ from the graph of f, bullet list and Example 2.6
findings.issue: The draft over-generalizes monotonicity into derivative sign.
findings.evidence: It states “where f is increasing… f′>0” and “where f is decreasing… f′<0”. A differentiable function can be increasing while having derivative 0 at some points, so this is false as a general claim.
findings.suggestion: Phrase this as a graphical/local slope-reading rule: where the drawn graph has upward-sloping tangents, f′>0; downward-sloping tangents, f′<0; horizontal tangents, f′=0. Avoid using formal monotonicity alone to infer strict derivative sign without extra assumptions.

findings.issue: The mandated graphical your-turn sketch task is missing.
findings.evidence: The brief specifies Exer 2.6 should give a new curve and ask students to sketch f′. The draft gives only a verbal monotonicity description and asks for positive/negative intervals and zeros.
findings.suggestion: Revise Exercise 2.6 so it presents a new curve or graph and asks for a sketch of f′; sign and zero questions may remain as scaffolding.

summary: Blocking findings remain: one domain inconsistency, one over-generalized graph/derivative claim, and one missing direction-brief graphical exercise requirement.
